architecture.summary: Dynamic KV Injection (DKI) is a training-free, inference-time memory system that injects user-level Key-Value representations directly into the attention mechanism of LLMs without consuming prompt tokens. The system uses Memory Influence Scaling (MIS) with strength control α ∈ [0, 1], Query-Conditioned Memory Projection via FiLM-style modulation, and Dual-Factor Gating combining uncertainty and relevance signals. A Hybrid Injection Strategy deploys negative-position K/V injection for stable preferences and suffix-prompt injection for dynamic session history. Information flows from stored user memory through query-conditioned projection into attention heads without token-level concatenation.

biblio.title: Dynamic KV Injection: An Attention-Level User Memory System for Large Language Models

claims.statement: DKI is designed specifically for user-level memory (personal preferences, session history, user context), not for external knowledge bases or public data retrieval
Preliminary experiments on PersonaChat show comparable memory recall with 43.7% reduction in subsequent-turn latency
DKI significantly outperforms RAG by +10-16% at user context lengths >2500 tokens
DKI injects Key-Value representations directly into the attention mechanism without consuming prompt tokens, enabling a paradigm shift from token-level to attention-level memory injection
Memory Influence Scaling (MIS) provides continuous strength control α ∈ [0, 1]
Query-Conditioned Memory Projection uses FiLM-style modulation; Dual-Factor Gating combines uncertainty and relevance signals
Attention Budget Reallocation Hypothesis: in reasoning-intensive tasks, the marginal benefit of releasing token budget exceeds the marginal cost of increased attention computation
